name: tox (Python Testing Automation & Virtual Env Matrix)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); tox-dev / Bernát Gábor et al.

performance.notes: Overhead is venv creation (one-time per env); test speed is subprocess-dependent

agent_readiness.notes: Run tests across multiple Python versions and environments. tox.ini or pyproject.toml. [tox] envlist = py39,py310,py311,lint. [testenv] deps = pytest; commands = pytest {posargs}. [testenv:lint] deps = ruff; commands = ruff check src/. tox run — run all envs. tox run -e py311 — single env. tox run -e lint,py311 — multiple envs. tox -p auto for parallel. Factors: {envname} = py310-django42. generative envs: py{39,310,311}-django{40,42}. tox4 rewrite: faster, pyproject.toml support, TOML config. passenv = CI,COVERAGE_*. setenv = PYTHONPATH=src. Use with CI: tox run -e py311 in GitHub Actions.

gotchas: tox 4 (current) has breaking changes vs tox 3 config syntax
Venv creation slow on first run; subsequent runs reuse venvs
Parallel mode (-p auto) can cause interleaved output; use -p 0 to disable
pyproject.toml support in tox4 changed config keys — read migration guide
best_when: Testing Python libraries across multiple Python versions
Running linting, formatting, and testing in isolated envs
CI/CD pipeline standardization across different environments
Open-source Python packages needing multi-version compatibility
avoid_when: When nox offers more flexibility for complex env logic
summary: tox automates Python testing across multiple environments. tox.ini: [tox] envlist = py39,py310,lint; [testenv] commands = pytest. tox run; tox run -e lint. Generative envs: py{310,311}-django{40,42}. MIT. tox4 supports pyproject.toml. Parallel: tox -p auto. Best for library compatibility testing across Python versions. Use nox for more dynamic env logic.
